title: Virgin Galactic Holdings Inc (SPCE) (Q1 2024) Earnings Call Transcript Highlights: Navigating Through Financial Turbulence Towards Commercial Success
text: Virgin Galactic reported Q1 2024 results showing early-stage commercial operations with significant challenges ahead. The company generated $2 million in revenue during the quarter, derived from spaceflight services and membership fees. Operating expenses improved to $113 million, down from $164 million year-over-year, though free cash flow remained negative at $126 million. The firm maintains a strong liquidity position with $867 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities. Management projects Q2 revenue around $3.5 million and anticipates reaching a $450 million annualized run rate following commercial deployment of two Delta-class spacecraft. Key highlights include increased flight capacity from the mothership VMS Eve and aggressive pricing strategies. The company expects ticket prices to average over $800,000 per seat on upcoming flights, though it must first clear a backlog of lower-priced bookings exceeding 600 tickets. The company identified 2024 as its peak cash burn year, with substantial capital expenditures expected in Q3 and Q4. Management noted ongoing legal disputes with Boeing regarding intellectual property claims but stated these matters would not materially impact operations.
